text.title: Beta-Cohort Recruitment Output Contract
text.summary: The recruitment process produces a tracked cohort with signed agreements and a weekly engagement log. Anything less is "we have some users trying it," not a beta cohort.

list.item: cohort-log.csv with one row per signed partner. Columns: partner_id, company, role, icp_score, screener_pass_date, signed_date, cadence_slot, last_touchpoint, engagement_score, status (active / at-risk / graduated / exited).
signed-docs/ folder containing the mutual NDA + expectations one-pager per partner, named partnerid-yyyy-mm-dd.pdf.
screener-archive/ with screener responses (qualified AND rejected) — useful for ICP refinement.
weekly-cohort-report.md rolling: cohort size, active count, at-risk count, top 3 themes from synchronous syncs, decisions made from feedback.
activation: partner completed first-value workflow in product (binary, dated).
cadence_hit_rate: completed syncs / scheduled syncs across the program (target >= 70%).
feedback_volume: written feedback items per partner per week (target >= 1).
graduation_rate: at program end, fraction of cohort that converted to paying or champion status.

antipattern.description: Partner added to cohort-log.csv without a screener_pass_date AND signed_date.
antipattern.reason: Bypasses r2 + r3. Adding "informally accepted" partners pollutes engagement metrics and creates legal ambiguity over confidential feedback.

antipattern.description: NDA signed but no expectations one-pager — the legal floor exists but the cadence/exit contract does not.
antipattern.reason: NDA-only beta cohorts disengage silently because no commitment was made on either side beyond confidentiality.

antipattern.description: Cohort size > 15 with one founder running it.
antipattern.reason: Past ~15 partners, the founder cannot maintain weekly synchronous touchpoints; engagement signal collapses into anecdote.

list.item: Every cohort-log row has a matching signed PDF in signed-docs/.
Rejected screeners are archived, not deleted (ICP feedback loop).
Cadence_hit_rate computed weekly; partners below 50% are moved to at-risk.
Exit/graduation criteria written in the expectations doc and quoted in weekly-cohort-report.md when triggered.
